mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.1 million units signals a strengthening housing market that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.1M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening industrial sector that reduces corporate profits and investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 100 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decrease in initial jobless claims below 250,000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 250K are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 5% signals a strengthening consumer sector that boosts corporate profits and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the GDP deflator below 2% signals a weakening inflationary pressure that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL < 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
